task_desc: Fix background tab causing perpetual stimulus and runaway drive accumulation -- the brain tick (setInterval at 500ms, main.js:226) continues running when the tab is backgrounded (browsers throttle to ~1/s), but requestAnimationFrame pauses so update() at main.js:1277 never executes. This causes three compounding problems: (1) touch and wind stimulus timers at main.js:1398-1410 only clear inside update(), so a touch or wind event that fires just before tab switch persists as BRAIN.stimulate.touch=true or wind=true indefinitely -- each brain tick then adds fear(+0.3) and groom(+0.2) for touch or fear(+0.2*windStrength) for wind, saturating drives at 1.0 within seconds, (2) foodNearby and foodContact flags at main.js:1360-1361 are only reset at the start of update(), so if food was near the fly when the tab was switched, olfactory (OLF_ORN_FOOD) and gustatory (GUS_GRN_SWEET) neurons fire every brain tick indefinitely, (3) hunger(+0.005/tick) and groom(+0.008/tick) drives accumulate naturally each tick without behavioral offset since the fly is not actually moving/feeding/grooming through the RAF loop; the combined effect is that returning after even 30 seconds produces maxed fear/hunger/groom drives causing an immediate jarring behavioral cascade (startle->fly->groom->feed). Fix: store the setInterval ID from main.js:226, add a document.addEventListener('visibilitychange') handler that calls clearInterval when document.hidden becomes true and restarts the interval when visible again; on resume, clear all stale stimuli (touch, wind, foodNearby, foodContact) before the first brain tick fires, and optionally cap the first post-resume drive deltas to prevent accumulated drift from the throttled ticks that ran before the pause took effect. Files: js/main.js.
